evals.prompt:
Create a review on PR #42 on janrito/rbtr. The PR node ID is PR_kwDOtest1234abc. The head commit SHA is a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2. There is no pending review yet. Here is the diff:

diff --git a/src/client.py b/src/client.py
index abc1234..def5678 100644
--- a/src/client.py
+++ b/src/client.py
@@ -40,3 +40,5 @@
 existing line 40
 existing line 41
+new line 42
+new line 43
 existing line 44
diff --git a/src/server.py b/src/server.py
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/src/server.py
+++ b/src/server.py
@@ -1,3 +1,2 @@
 keep this
-remove this
 and this

Add a comment about missing error handling on the new line 42 of src/client.py.
evals.expected_output: A gh api graphql command that creates a pending review with one thread targeting line 42 of src/client.py on the RIGHT side.
expectations: Reads or references queries/add_review.graphql for the mutation syntax
Uses --input - (piped JSON) rather than -f for the threads array, because -f passes arrays as string literals
Sets pullRequestId to PR_kwDOtest1234abc (the PR node ID, not a review ID)
Sets commitOID to a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2 (the head SHA)
Thread has path set to src/client.py
Thread has line set to 42
Thread has side set to RIGHT (line 42 is a + line in the diff)

evals.prompt:
There is already a pending review on this PR with review node ID PRR_kwDOtest5678xyz. Add another comment about poor naming on line 2 of src/server.py. Here is the diff:

diff --git a/src/client.py b/src/client.py
index abc1234..def5678 100644
--- a/src/client.py
+++ b/src/client.py
@@ -40,3 +40,5 @@
 existing line 40
 existing line 41
+new line 42
+new line 43
 existing line 44
diff --git a/src/server.py b/src/server.py
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/src/server.py
+++ b/src/server.py
@@ -1,3 +1,2 @@
 keep this
-remove this
 and this

The comment is about the removed line 'remove this'.
evals.expected_output: A gh api graphql command that adds a thread to the existing pending review, targeting line 2 of src/server.py on the LEFT side.
expectations: Reads or references queries/add_comment.graphql for the mutation syntax
Uses pullRequestReviewId (not pullRequestId) set to PRR_kwDOtest5678xyz
Sets path to src/server.py
Sets line to 2 (the deleted line in the server.py hunk)
Sets side to LEFT (line 2 is a - line, only commentable on the old-file side)

evals.prompt: Submit the pending review with ID PRR_kwDOtest5678xyz as a comment (not approval, not request changes).
evals.expected_output: A gh api graphql command that submits the review with event COMMENT.
expectations: Reads or references queries/submit_review.graphql for the mutation syntax
Sets pullRequestReviewId to PRR_kwDOtest5678xyz
Sets event to COMMENT (not APPROVE or REQUEST_CHANGES)

evals.prompt: Approve PR #42. The pending review ID is PRR_kwDOtest5678xyz.
evals.expected_output: A gh api graphql command that submits the review with event APPROVE.
expectations: Reads or references queries/submit_review.graphql for the mutation syntax
Sets event to APPROVE

evals.prompt:
Comment on the newly added line in this diff. The PR node ID is PR_kwDOtest1234abc, head SHA is a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2, and there is no pending review yet.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 context line 1
+added line
 context line 2
 context line 3

The file path is src/utils.py. Say something about the added line.
evals.expected_output: A gh api graphql command that creates a review with a thread on line 2 (the + line), RIGHT side, of src/utils.py.
expectations: Sets line to 2 (the added line is at new-file line 2)
Sets side to RIGHT
Sets path to src/utils.py

evals.prompt:
Comment on the removed line in this diff. The pending review ID is PRR_kwDOtest5678xyz.

@@ -10,4 +10,3 @@
 context before
-deleted line
 context after
 more context

The file path is src/handler.py. Comment on why this line was deleted.
evals.expected_output: A gh api graphql command that adds a thread on line 11 (the - line), LEFT side, of src/handler.py.
expectations: Sets line to 11 (the deleted line is at old-file line 11)
Sets side to LEFT
Sets path to src/handler.py

evals.prompt:
Comment on the second addition in this multi-hunk diff. The pending review ID is PRR_kwDOtest5678xyz.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 first context
+first addition
 first end
@@ -20,3 +21,4 @@
 second context
+second addition
 second end

The file path is src/config.py. Comment on the second addition (not the first).
evals.expected_output: A gh api graphql command that adds a thread on line 22 (the + line in the second hunk), RIGHT side.
expectations: Sets line to 22 (the second addition is at new-file line 22 in the second hunk)
Sets side to RIGHT
Does NOT target line 2 (that would be the first addition)

evals.prompt:
Comment on line 999 of src/utils.py. The pending review ID is PRR_kwDOtest5678xyz. Here is the diff for that file:

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 context line 1
+added line
 context line 2
 context line 3

Add a comment about the function on line 999.
evals.expected_output: The LLM recognises that line 999 is not within the diff hunk (which covers lines 1-4) and either explains the error or picks a valid line.
expectations: Recognises that line 999 is not commentable (the diff hunk only covers lines 1-4)
Either explains that only lines within diff hunks can be commented on, or suggests a valid alternative line
Does NOT blindly construct a command with line=999

evals.prompt:
Suggest replacing the added line in this diff with `improved_line()`. The pending review ID is PRR_kwDOtest5678xyz.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 context line 1
+added line
 context line 2
 context line 3

The file path is src/utils.py.
evals.expected_output: A gh api graphql command where the comment body contains a ```suggestion block replacing the single line, targeting line 2 RIGHT side.
expectations: Comment body contains a ```suggestion code fence block
The suggestion block contains improved_line() as the replacement
Targets line 2 on the RIGHT side (single-line suggestion)
Does NOT set startLine (single-line suggestions don't need it)

evals.prompt:
Suggest replacing lines 42-43 of src/client.py with a single call `do_both()`. The pending review ID is PRR_kwDOtest5678xyz. Here is the diff:

diff --git a/src/client.py b/src/client.py
index abc1234..def5678 100644
--- a/src/client.py
+++ b/src/client.py
@@ -40,3 +40,5 @@
 existing line 40
 existing line 41
+new line 42
+new line 43
 existing line 44
evals.expected_output: A gh api graphql command where the comment body contains a ```suggestion block, with startLine=42 and line=43, both on the RIGHT side.
expectations: Comment body contains a ```suggestion code fence block
The suggestion block contains do_both() as the replacement
Sets startLine to 42 and line to 43 (inclusive range covering both + lines)
Both startLine and line are on the RIGHT side
Sets startSide to match side (both RIGHT)

evals.prompt:
I tried to add a comment on line 50 of src/utils.py but got this error from GitHub:

pull_request_review_thread.line must be part of the diff

Here is the diff for that file:

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 context line 1
+added line
 context line 2
 context line 3

What went wrong and what should I do?
evals.expected_output: Explanation that line 50 is outside the diff hunk (lines 1-4), and suggestion to pick a valid line within the hunk.
expectations: Identifies that line 50 is outside the diff hunk
Explains that only lines within diff hunks are commentable
Suggests specific valid lines from the diff (lines 1-4 on RIGHT, or 1-3 on LEFT)
Does NOT suggest retrying with the same line number

evals.prompt:
I'm trying to create a review and I'm getting this error:

Could not resolve to a node with the global id of '12345'

I used -f prId=12345 in my gh api graphql command. The PR number is 42. What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Explanation that 12345 is a numeric ID but GraphQL mutations require node IDs (base64 strings like PR_kwDO...).
expectations: Identifies that 12345 is a numeric/database ID, not a GraphQL node ID
Explains that node IDs are base64 strings (mentions the PR_kwDO... or similar prefix pattern)
Suggests querying for the node ID first (e.g. using pr_id.graphql or equivalent query)
Does NOT suggest retrying with the same numeric ID

evals.prompt:
I'm running gh api graphql to create a review but getting:

gh: authentication required

How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of how to authenticate the gh CLI.
expectations: Suggests running gh auth login to authenticate interactively
Mentions GH_TOKEN or GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable as an alternative
Does NOT suggest modifying the GraphQL query itself

evals.prompt: I need to review PR #42 on janrito/rbtr — it's a refactor of the auth module. Can you walk me through how to get started? I haven't looked at it yet.
evals.expected_output: The agent directs to read the diff, then fetch PR context (description, existing threads) and node IDs before writing any comments.
expectations: References gh pr diff to read the diff
References fetch_pr.graphql to read existing threads and PR description
References pr_id.graphql for node IDs and pending review status
Mentions reading context (description, existing threads) BEFORE any mention of add_review.graphql or add_comment.graphql
Does NOT jump straight to creating or adding comments without reading first

evals.prompt: I started reviewing PR #42 on janrito/rbtr yesterday and left a bunch of comments. My pending review ID is PRR_kwDOtest5678xyz. I want to pick up where I left off — how do I see what I've already commented on?
evals.expected_output: The agent uses fetch_review_comments.graphql with the review node ID, explaining that reviewThreads may not show all pending comments.
expectations: References fetch_review_comments.graphql with the review node ID
Explains that reviewThreads (fetch_pr.graphql) may miss pending comments, especially on newly added files
Sets reviewId to PRR_kwDOtest5678xyz
Does NOT suggest relying solely on fetch_pr.graphql reviewThreads for pending comments

evals.prompt: ok I've finished adding comments to my pending review PRR_kwDOtest5678xyz on PR #42 on janrito/rbtr. ready to submit — anything I should check first?
evals.expected_output: The agent advises re-fetching pending comments and existing threads to verify state before submitting.
expectations: Advises re-reading pending comments via fetch_review_comments.graphql before submitting
Advises re-fetching existing threads via fetch_pr.graphql to catch new activity
Mentions the verification steps BEFORE referencing submit_review.graphql
Does NOT jump straight to submit_review.graphql without a verification step

evals.prompt: I'm reviewing PR #42 on janrito/rbtr. I ran fetch_pr.graphql and there's already a thread (ID PRT_kwDOtest9999abc) on line 42 of src/client.py where janrito flagged missing error handling. I noticed the same issue and want to add my thoughts. What's the right way to do this?
evals.expected_output: The agent suggests replying to the existing thread using add_thread_reply.graphql rather than creating a duplicate thread.
expectations: References add_thread_reply.graphql to reply to the existing thread
Uses the thread node ID PRT_kwDOtest9999abc
Does NOT create a new thread on the same line about the same topic via add_review.graphql or add_comment.graphql
Explains that replying to an existing thread is preferable to starting a duplicate
